utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi jerry , how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good to see you as well . um , so i know that the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: jerry is a 54 year old male with a past medical history , significant for osteoporosis and multiple sclerosis who presents for an annual exam . so jerry , what's been going on since the last time i saw you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , we have been traveling all over the country . it's been kind of a stressful summer . kinda adjusting to everything in the fall and so far it's been good , but ah , lack of sleep , it's been really getting to me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and have you taken anything for the insomnia . have you tried any strategies for it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've tried everything from melatonin to meditation to , uh , t- stretching out every morning when i get up . nothing really seems to help though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: in terms of your osteoporosis , i know we have you on fosamax , any issues with your joints , any issues like-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no broken bones recently ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no , nothing like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then in terms of your multiple sclerosis , when was the last time you saw the neurologist ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , about six months ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you're taking the medication ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any issues with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none whatsoever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and any additional weakness ? i know you were having some issues with your right leg , but that seems to have improved or ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes a lingering issue with my knee surgery . but other than that it's been fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: pretty , pretty strong , n- nothing , nothing out of the ordinary .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , well i know you did a review of systems sheet when you checked in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you were endorsing that insomnia . any other issues , chest pain , shortness of breath , anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well lets go ahead and do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the vital signs . so your vital signs here in the office they look really good . i'm just going to listen to your heart and lungs and let you know what i find .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . on physical examination everything looks good . you know your lungs are nice and clear . your heart sounds good . you know you do have some weakness of your lower extremities . the right is about 4 out of 5 , the left is about 3 out of 5 . but you reflexes are really good so i'm , i'm encouraged by that . and you do have some , you know , arthritic changes of the right knee .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so let's go over some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the right knee x-ray . and here's the x-ray of your right knee , which shows some changes from arthritis , but otherwise that looks good . so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan . from an osteoporosis standpoint , we'll go ahead and order , you know , re- continue on the fosamax . do you need a refill on that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order a refill of fosamax 1 tab per week , 11 refills . and then in t- , for your second problem , your multiple sclerosis i want you to go ahead and continue to see the neurologist and continue on those medications . and let me know if you need anything from that standpoint , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at this point , no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 54-year-old male with a history of osteoporosis and multiple sclerosis presenting for an annual exam.
Annual exam
The patient reports recent travel, stress, and insomnia.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried any strategies for insomnia.
The patient has tried melatonin, meditation, and morning stretching for insomnia without success.
Osteoporosis
The doctor asks if the patient has any joint issues.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any recent broken bones.
Multiple Sclerosis
The doctor asks when the patient last saw the neurologist.
The patient last saw the neurologist six months ago.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking their medication.
The doctor asks if the patient has any issues with their medication.
The doctor asks about any additional weakness in the legs.
The patient reports lingering issues with his knee after surgery.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain or shortness of breath.
The doctor declares the patient's vital signs are good.
Physical exam shows clear lungs, good heart sounds, lower extremity weakness (4/5 right, 3/5 left), good reflexes, and arthritic changes in the right knee.
Right knee x-ray shows arthritic changes.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill on Fosamax.
The doctor orders a refill of Fosamax 1 tab per week with 11 refills.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue seeing the neurologist and continue current medications for multiple sclerosis.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
